notes: This registry is a first-pass extraction for tooling and scoped prompt loading.
implementation_status values: implemented, partial, planned, manual_review

rules.summary: Notation is governed by block role; formal bodies and standard/negated/contrapositive blocks use standard notation, while predicate readings may use canonical predicates.
checks: forbid canonical predicates in standard, negated, and contrapositive quantified statements
allow canonical predicates in predicate-reading and explicit predicate-analysis blocks
generation_contract: always state the target role in the prompt
include role-specific forbidden/allowed notation constraints

rules.summary: Predicate names from predicates.yaml must not appear in formal bodies or standard/negated/contrapositive quantified statements.
checks: report predicate hits with file, line, env, and label
generation_contract: do not use \operatorname{...} in forbidden roles
use ordinary mathematical notation in forbidden roles

rules.summary: Before accepting transformed formal output, report block type, notation family, and compliance.
checks: emit compliance object per generated block
fail output if predicate leakage occurs
generation_contract: generated output must pass a post-generation role audit before assembly

rules.summary: Repository has canonical top-level layout including common files, predicates, notation, relations, and volumes.
checks: verify expected top-level files and directories exist

rules.summary: Each volume has a defined mathematical scope.
checks: flag chapter paths whose subject appears outside the volume scope
generation_contract: place new chapters in the volume matching their subject scope

rules.summary: Volume IV is excluded from card extraction by default.
Each volume declares canonical chapter subjects in dependency order.
checks: verify generated chapter subject appears in registry
generation_contract: do not invent chapter subjects outside the registry

rules.summary: Chapter registry order is dependency order, not alphabetical order.
checks: use registry order for breadcrumbs and neighbor discovery
generation_contract: preserve registry order

rules.summary: Chapter subject names are repository identifiers distinct from display titles.
checks: validate path subject names separately from rendered titles
generation_contract: use repository subject in paths and display title in rendered text

rules.summary: Every chapter follows the canonical index, notes, proofs, exercises, and capstone structure.
generation_contract: create canonical chapter skeletons

rules.summary: Root main.tex inputs volumes only; chapter index is the only file that inputs proofs/index.tex; section directories do not contain local index.tex.
figure-*.tex files contain TikZ code only, no document preamble, and dependency graphs are stored in figure files rather than inline notes.
checks: flag preamble commands in figure files
flag inline TikZ dependency graphs in notes
generation_contract: place dependency figures in dedicated figure files

rules.summary: Stub generation is first-class and creates canonical minimum structure without inventing mathematics silently.
generation_contract: use placeholders when scope is unclear
do not invent theorem lists silently

rules.summary: Every chapter has exactly one capstone exercise, last in proofs/exercises/index.tex.
generation_contract: create capstone-<chapter>.tex

rules.source: DESIGN.md 3.1, Canonical Predicate Relation Notation Sources
rules.summary: All predicate, relation, and notation names come from predicates.yaml, relations.yaml, and notation.yaml.
generation_contract: do not invent local predicates, relations, or notation names

rules.summary: Imported content is rewritten into house notation before entering notes.
checks: manual or model-assisted notation conformity review
generation_contract: prefer repository notation over source notation

rules.summary: If a quantified logical structure needs a missing predicate, add an audit entry rather than inventing one inline.
generation_contract: return missing_predicate rather than inventing a predicate

rules.summary: Quantified forms follow canonical styles in notation.yaml, such as n >= N for sequence tails.
generation_contract: use canonical quantifier patterns from notation.yaml

rules.summary: Local deviation from canonical notation requires permission.
checks: flag deviations from canonical notation for review
generation_contract: do not intentionally deviate without explicit instruction

rules.summary: Allowed box types are Definition, Axiom, Theorem, and Toolkit boxes with fixed palette.
generation_contract: use canonical box vocabulary only

rules.summary: Every non-toolkit box wraps a single bare formal environment; boxes must not contain ad hoc prose masquerading as formal content.
checks: count formal environments inside non-toolkit tcolorbox
generation_contract: do not put prose lists inside non-toolkit formal boxes

rules.summary: Boxes are scarce and structural; a section ordinarily moves through Toolkit, first boxed definition, exposition, bare theorem/proposition, proof, and remarks.
generation_contract: avoid decorative boxing

rules.summary: Every chapter index opens with breadcrumb box followed by structural roadmap.
generation_contract: generate breadcrumb then roadmap

rules.summary: Breadcrumb title is chapter subject, shows immediate conceptual neighborhood, uses arrows, bolds current chapter, and fits in the box.
generation_contract: derive neighbors from registry

rules.summary: Every definition or theorem-like environment contains exactly one independently extractable mathematical item.
generation_contract: one item, one environment, one label

rules.summary: Bundled content includes lists, multiple concepts, multiple operations, multi-line definitions, conditional clauses, and inline definition chains.
generation_contract: return split_environment request when detected

rules.summary: Bundled content must be repaired using Toolkit box, exposition, then atomic formal blocks.
checks: detect relevant Toolkit before decomposed family
generation_contract: when splitting a family, generate or verify Toolkit and exposition before formal blocks

rules.summary: No formatting choice exempts bundled content from decomposition.
checks: apply bundled-content checks independent of formatting style
generation_contract: do not preserve bundles for brevity or tradition

rules.summary: Every theorem-like or definition environment has a label.
generation_contract: put exactly one label immediately after environment heading

rules.summary: Labels use prefixes matching environment kind.
generation_contract: use correct label prefix

rules.summary: Labels are unique across the relevant analysis target.
checks: flag duplicate labels with all locations
generation_contract: do not reuse an existing label

rules.summary: Formal environments are followed immediately by logical blocks in canonical order.
generation_contract: assemble generated blocks in DESIGN order

rules.summary: Every definition and theorem-like environment is followed by a Standard quantified statement.
checks: verify nearby Standard quantified statement remark
generation_contract: generate standard notation only

rules.summary: Predicate-reading headers are role-specific, not generic.
generation_contract: use Definition predicate reading, Negation predicate reading, or Contrapositive predicate reading

rules.summary: Negated quantified statement contains formal negation only; explanatory prose belongs later.
generation_contract: formal negation only

rules.summary: Important negations may be followed by Failure mode decomposition, possibly with underbraces or visual grouping.
checks: detect generated failure modes without decomposition when complex
generation_contract: use aligned displays for multi-branch decomposition

rules.summary: Distinct concepts are not collapsed into one predicate block unless canonical predicate library treats them as inseparable.
checks: detect multiple distinct predicate families in one predicate reading
generation_contract: one predicate reading per atomic item

rules.summary: Contrapositive blocks are included only when mathematically illuminating.
checks: require explicit omit reason when skipped or included
generation_contract: return omit JSON when not useful

rules.summary: Long or multi-clause logical statements use aligned environments with breaks at implications, conjunctions, and disjunctions.
generation_contract: use aligned for long or multi-clause displays

rules.summary: Every quantified variable in logical blocks carries an explicit domain unless fixed in the immediately preceding statement; no free variables.
generation_contract: declare domains for newly quantified variables

rules.summary: When quantifying over a sequence, declare the ambient space.
checks: flag sequence quantifiers without ambient space
generation_contract: write forms like \forall (x_n) \subseteq \mathbb{R}

rules.summary: Predicate statements representing full theorem/definition should be logically closed rather than leaving parameters free.
checks: flag free parameters in predicate readings when not fixed
generation_contract: close predicate readings or identify fixed parameters

rules.summary: Expository prose explains; formal mathematical content must already appear in formal environments or logical blocks.
generation_contract: do not introduce new formal content in prose

rules.summary: Every definition and theorem-like environment carries an Interpretation block by default, with limited shared-exposition exceptions.
checks: verify attached Interpretation or documented shared exposition exception
generation_contract: generate Interpretation unless explicit shared-exposition exception applies

rules.summary: Interpretation blocks are prose only and do not use formal predicate language unless explicitly discussing notation.
generation_contract: write concise explanatory prose

rules.summary: When itemize begins immediately inside remark*, insert \hfill after the heading.
checks: flag remark* directly followed by itemize without \hfill
generation_contract: include \hfill before immediate itemize

rules.summary: Dependency-figure node colors reinforce box vocabulary.
checks: scan TikZ styles for node type colors
generation_contract: use shared node color styles

rules.summary: Dependency edge colors are standardized: implies blue, uses yellow, defines green.
generation_contract: use standardized relation colors

rules.summary: Dependency figures include legend, explanatory caption, label, display titles, bottom legend macro, and unlabeled edges by default.
checks: flag raw label IDs as visible node labels
generation_contract: use standard bottom legend macro and explanatory caption

rules.summary: Figure files contain no inline color definitions.
generation_contract: use common/colors.tex shared palette

rules.summary: Theorem-like statements live in notes; proofs live in separate proof files; no inline proofs in notes unless example-designated.
checks: flag proof environments in notes files
verify proof file exists when required
generation_contract: do not generate inline proofs in notes

rules.summary: Generated proofs initially must be stubs containing exactly TODO.
checks: verify generated proof stub body exactly TODO
generation_contract: use \begin{proof} TODO \end{proof} only until user-approved proof replacement

rules.source: DESIGN.md Proof Replacement Lifecycle, No Premature Proof Generation
rules.summary: Proof stubs are replaced only after user-authored proof is reviewed and accepted; do not generate full proofs prematurely.
generation_contract: do not generate full proof unless explicitly requested after user proof

rules.summary: Each theorem has exactly one proof file.
checks: map theorem labels to proof files
flag missing or duplicate proof files
generation_contract: create one proof file per theorem label

rules.source: DESIGN.md Proof Navigation Linking, Theorem and Proof Navigation Links
rules.summary: Theorem-like statements include Go to proof links, and proof files include Return links.
checks: scan proof files for Return hyperref[thm:]
generation_contract: include bidirectional navigation

rules.summary: Proof files use canonical stack: header, Return, restatement, professional proof, learning proof, proof structure, dependencies.
generation_contract: preserve canonical proof-file stack

rules.summary: Proof-file restatements use unnumbered theorem-like environments and do not contain labels inside restatement environments.
checks: flag numbered theorem environments in proof files
generation_contract: use theorem*, lemma*, proposition*, or corollary* restatements

rules.summary: Proof files use ordinary environments and hyperlinks only; no flash macros or step macros.
checks: scan proof files for forbidden proof/flash macros
generation_contract: use bold inline step headings only in detailed proof

rules.summary: Proof files distinguish professional standard proof from detailed learning proof.
checks: verify two proof environments with required headings
generation_contract: include both layers when full proof generation is authorized

rules.summary: Default proof output is high fidelity unless compact/proof-only mode is explicitly requested.
generation_contract: include house notation, labeled statement, professional proof, learning proof, explanatory prose, and no forbidden macros by default

rules.summary: Standardization planning preserves original files and writes sidecar old/tmp/audit/request files before replacement.
generation_contract: do not replace source without approval
